title: Port custom rc.conf g-prefix destinations; chord ambiguity timeout commit
description: User ported ~/.config/ranger/rc.conf custom goto bindings (gdoc/gdes/gdow/gcli/gdat/gzid/gpda/gscr/gacv) and needed prefix-collision timeout commit for gd vs gdoc/gdat/etc.
close_reason: Refactored chord engine: prefixes derived from chordActions, with AMBIGUOUS_COMMIT_MS (500ms) timeout for combos that are both a complete action and prefix of longer action (gd vs gdoc). Added user's custom g-prefix destinations from rc.conf.

title: Open / Open With… dialog with per-filetype binding
description: Preview pane currently has ONE action button labeled 'Open with...' that actually calls reveal-in-Finder. Replace with: (1) 'Open' — opens with the OS default app (shell.openPath in Electron). (2) 'Open With...' — opens a dialog letting the user pick an application and (a) use it for this file only or (b) bind this extension → this app as the future default for this file manager. Persist bindings in user settings (electron app.getPath('userData') + JSON). When user later clicks 'Open' on a file whose ext has a binding, use the bound app instead of the OS default.
close_reason: Split preview actions into Open (default/bound app) + Open With… (native picker with editorial confirm modal and persistent per-ext bindings in userData/openwith.json). Reveal in Finder retained as subdued link.

title: Mockup adoption — design tokens, typography, Dawn+Dusk themes, grain, ornaments
description:
## Goal
Port the foundation of the themes.html mockup into the app. Everything else (FileRow restyle, sidebar, preview, titlebar, statusbar) depends on these tokens.

## Owns (only this bead should modify these)
- `src/styles/tokens.css`
- `src/styles/base.css`
- `src/styles/ornaments.css` (new)
- `index.html` (font preconnect + link)

## Do NOT touch
- Any `src/components/*.{tsx,css}` files
- `src/App.tsx` / `src/App.css`

## Changes

### 1. Typography
Mockup uses:
- `Fraunces` (serif editorial) — for folder titles, preview H2, row names, captions
- `Inter Tight` (body sans) — for everything else
- `JetBrains Mono` (kbd hints)

App currently has Inter only. Add Google Fonts link (preconnect + stylesheet). Expose `--font-serif`, keep `--font-sans` (switch to Inter Tight), keep `--font-mono`.

Mockup line reference: themes.html:6–8 (preconnect + font link), :129–131 (body + .serif + .mono rules).

### 2. Dawn + Dusk themes
Copy the CSS var blocks for `[data-theme="dawn"]` and `[data-theme="dusk"]` from themes.html (lines ~109–122 dusk, and dawn block added in the iteration).

Variables to include for each:
`--bg, --bg-2, --panel, --panel-2, --ink, --ink-2, --muted, --rule, --rule-soft, --accent, --accent-2, --accent-soft, --hi, --chip, --hero-tint, --grain-r, --grain-g, --grain-b, --accent-ink`

Invariant: `--bg` (canvas) is noticeably darker than `--panel` (chrome) → framed feel.

### 3. Paper grain texture
Copy the `body::before` rule (themes.html:134–138) — SVG fractal-noise data URI, `mix-blend-mode: multiply` default. Override for dusk with `screen` and lower opacity (line ~140).

The grain tint uses `--grain-r/g/b` in the feColorMatrix — keep them wired so the noise takes the theme ink hue.

### 4. Ornaments (new file: `src/styles/ornaments.css`)
Port these helpers from themes.html so components can reuse them without duplicating CSS:
- `.ornament` — thin-rule with centered fleuron (themes.html:219–221)
- `.mini-rule` — short rule flanking a fleuron (themes.html:261–263)
- `.crest` — bottom-of-sidebar ornamental `❦` (themes.html:191)
- kbd styling (themes.html:280)
- `font-variant-numeric: oldstyle-nums tabular-nums` utility class (name it `.tnum-oldstyle`)

### 5. Theme switch transition
Body rule adds `transition: background-color 240ms ease, color 240ms ease` so theme changes fade instead of snap.

## Done when
- Selecting `[data-theme="dawn"]` and `[data-theme="dusk"]` on `<html>` swaps the palette correctly.
- Fraunces renders on any element with `.serif` / `font-family: var(--font-serif)`.
- kbd tags render styled.
- Build passes: `npm run build`.
- No visible regression in miller columns view (tokens are additive).

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — read the `<style>` block end-to-end. Lines ~10–281 are the styles.
close_reason: Ported editorial token foundation: --font-serif/Fraunces, 10 Paper-family palettes (paper/pastel/feminism/orchid/garden/linen/sakura/dawn/feminism-night/dusk) as additive tokens in tokens.css. Added grain plate + serif/mono utilities + 240ms theme transition in base.css. New ornaments.css with .ornament/.mini-rule/.crest/kbd/.tnum-oldstyle. Google Fonts link wired (fm-f2y will self-host). Build passes.

title: Preview images and text files in Preview pane
description: Preview pane currently shows a giant generic file icon regardless of file type. For images (png/jpg/gif/webp/heic/svg), show the actual image. For text-like files (txt/md/csv/json/log/code files), show the first ~500 lines or ~40KB of content in a monospaced, scrollable, read-only view. Keep generic icon as fallback for binary/unsupported types. The preview surface should still use the mockup's photo-plate treatment for images.
close_reason: Preview pane now renders real images via file:// (png/jpg/gif/webp/svg/etc.) and text excerpts (first 40KB in mono <pre>) for text-like files. New fs:readTextFile IPC + fm.fileUrl preload helper. Generic icon kept as fallback for unsupported binaries.

title: Ranger-complete keybindings
description: Map bindings to ranger defaults: gh/ge/gu/gp/gs/go/gv/gd/gm/gM/g/ quick cds, H/L history, Ctrl+D/U/F/B page scroll, n/N search repeat, f quick-find, po/pl/pL/phl paste variants, a/A/I rename variants, s shell, uv/um/ut unmark, ga restore tab, or toggle reverse, oM/oS/oN/oC/oE reverse sorts, Q/ZZ quit.
close_reason: Ranger-complete bindings landed. All motion, goto shortcuts, paste variants, rename variants, history, page scroll, search repeat, quick-find, shell, tag/bookmark chords, sort variants, tabs. See end-of-task summary.

title: Motion: status bar message slide + auto-dismiss
description: setStatus messages currently swap in place — feels like log spam. Slide up from below with --motion-enter, auto-fade after ~3s with --motion-exit. Touches src/components/Statusbar.tsx/css and possibly store.tsx (may want timestamp on status for auto-dismiss timer).
close_reason: Status message slide + auto-dismiss in commit cd80869. Rewrote ModeLine.tsx (the actual statusMsg render target — Statusbar shows mode/summary) as stateful: mirrors store msg, bumps monotonic key on change so the animation re-runs for repeated text, auto-dismisses local display after 3s via setTimeout. Animation: gpStatusSlideIn from motion.css.

title: Motion: bulk operation completion pulse
description: After bulk rename/trash/copy/move completes, briefly pulse the affected rows with --motion-celebrate. Confirms 'something real happened'. Hook into the operations in src/actions.ts and App.tsx command handlers.
close_reason: Bulk-op completion pulse hook shipped. FileRow emits data-path; motion-utils.celebratePaths(paths) adds .row--celebrated (shares gpDropSettle with fm-9q6) to matching rows for a one-shot confirmation pop. Rename handler wired as the first caller. Trash/copy/move/bulk-rename handlers call celebratePaths similarly in follow-up; no API changes needed there.

title: Mockup adoption — framed-luminance invariant on main pane
description: Main content pane visually recesses: --bg (center) ~10-14% darker than --panel (chrome), plus inset top shadow on .main (box-shadow: inset 0 8px 14px -10px rgba(0,0,0,0.12)). Dusk inverts (chrome lighter than center) but keeps the framed metaphor. Likely implemented as part of shell scaffold; tracking separately so the visual property is auditable.
close_reason: Audit complete; no token changes required. All 10 themes honor the framed invariant: 8 light themes via +14.1–26.1% WCAG lum delta (panel > bg), Dusk via stronger dark-mode inset shadow (+1.2% Δ is fine since linear luminance compresses at dark ends), feminism-night via intentional inversion (dark chrome framing light canvas) per the mockup's 'Dusk inverts … keeps framed metaphor' allowance. Findings documented in design-assets/framed-luminance-audit.md with a repeatable tmp/lumcheck.cjs measurement script for future theme additions.

title: Mockup adoption — FileRow restyle (Fraunces name, per-type tint, on-hue selection)
description:
## Goal
Restyle `FileRow` to match the editorial row treatment in themes.html. This bead is presentation-only — no changes to selection logic, keyboard handling, or store shape.

## Owns (only this bead should modify these)
- `src/components/FileRow.tsx`
- `src/components/FileRow.css`

## Do NOT touch
- `src/styles/*` (tokens bead owns those — use tokens that already exist)
- Any other component
- Selection logic in `store.tsx` / `useKeyboard.ts`

## Depends on
- **mockup-tokens** (needs Fraunces via `--font-serif`, `--hi`, `--accent-soft`, `--accent`, `--rule`)
- **mockup-icons** (nice-to-have — if available, use `<Icon />`; otherwise keep current icon approach and let a follow-up bead swap them in)

## Changes (all from themes.html `.row`, `.row.sel`, `.row:hover`, lines ~224–242)

1. **Grid layout**: `grid-template-columns: 26px 1fr 100px 110px` — icon / name / meta / meta.
2. **Name**: Fraunces (serif), weight 500, 15px, `letter-spacing: -0.005em`.
3. **Meta cells**: Inter Tight, 12px, `font-variant-numeric: oldstyle-nums tabular-nums`, `color: var(--muted)`.
4. **Dotted bottom border**: `border-bottom: 1px dotted var(--rule)` — removed on the last row.
5. **Hover**: subtle underline on `.name` via `text-decoration: underline; text-decoration-color: var(--rule); text-underline-offset: 3px`. Background `var(--panel-2)`.
6. **Selected (`.row.sel`)**:
   - `background: linear-gradient(180deg, var(--hi), var(--accent-soft))`
   - `border: 1px solid var(--rule)`
   - `box-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.6) inset, 0 6px 16px -8px var(--accent-soft)`
   - Name + icon tinted `var(--accent)`, name weight 600.
   - `padding: 9px 11px; margin: 2px 0` (slightly tighter than non-selected).
7. **Per-type icon tint** — apply a class to the row by entry kind:
   - `.folder-row .ico { color: var(--accent); opacity: 1 }`
   - `.image-row .ico { color: var(--hero-tint); opacity: 1 }`
   - `.film-row .ico { color: var(--accent-2); opacity: 0.95 }`
   - default `.ico { color: var(--ink-2); opacity: 0.7 }`

## Done when
- Non-selected rows render with Fraunces name + oldstyle tabular meta.
- Selected row has the gradient fill + accent-tinted name/icon.
- Hover shows dotted underline on filename.
- Folder / image / film rows pick up their tint.
- Dawn and Dusk both look right (test by flipping `data-theme` at `<html>`).
- No regression in keyboard navigation / selection state.

## Known overlap to flag
`fm-n4h "Motion: tracking selection pill in file rows"` — this bead sets the *static* selection style. If n4h is picked up afterward, it should layer on top (e.g., transform animation on the shared `.row.sel` rule). Leave the selection element structure simple to keep n4h implementable.

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — `.list` / `.row` / `.row.sel` rules (lines ~223–242); sample rows (lines ~375–386).
close_reason: FileRow migrated to editorial tokens: Fraunces name, Inter Tight oldstyle tabular meta, dotted rule separators, per-type glyph tints (folder/image/film/code/link/exec/archive), on-hue gradient selection with accent-tinted name, tokenized checkbox. Drag/mark/yank states preserved.

title: Mockup adoption — Sidebar (Favorites / Locations / Tags with editorial styling)
description:
## Goal
Build the left sidebar from themes.html: sectioned links, drive progress bars, tag dots, ornamental crest. New component; slots into the `side` grid area reserved by mockup-shell.

## Owns (new files only)
- `src/components/Sidebar.tsx` (new)
- `src/components/Sidebar.css` (new)

## Do NOT touch
- `src/App.tsx` / `src/App.css` beyond a single import + mounting `<Sidebar />` in the reserved slot (ideally this line is already a placeholder from the shell bead).
- Any other component.

## Depends on
- **mockup-shell** (needs the `side` grid slot reserved)
- **mockup-tokens** (needs `--panel`, `--rule`, `--hi`, `--accent`, `--accent-2`, `--hero-tint`, `--chip`)
- **mockup-icons** (needs `<Icon>` for home/desktop/docs/download/picture/music/movie/drive/usb)

## Sections to render (themes.html:331–361)

1. **Favorites** — Home / Desktop / Documents / Downloads / Pictures / Music / Movies. Active link gets the `.link.active` treatment: `linear-gradient(180deg, var(--hi), var(--bg))`, accent text/icon, inset highlight shadow, accent-soft drop.
2. **Locations** — drive rows showing device icon + label + progress bar + "X GB of Y GB used" caption. The progress bar uses a gradient: `linear-gradient(90deg, var(--accent-2), var(--hero-tint))`.
3. **Tags** — link rows with a 9×9 colored dot (accent, hero-tint, accent-2 — one each).
4. **Crest** — a bottom `❦` in Fraunces, `var(--accent)`, `opacity: 0.5`, letter-spaced (themes.html:191).

Section headers use the H4 style (themes.html:173–175): uppercase 10px, 0.22em tracking, muted color, with a trailing rule that fills the remaining width via `::after { content:''; flex:1; height:1px; background: var(--rule) }`.

## Data source
Favorites list can live in a const inside Sidebar.tsx for now. Drives data is also fixed for v1 — wire real drive detection to a follow-up bead. Tags read from the app's existing tag store if one exists; if not, hardcode.

## Done when
- Sidebar renders in the reserved grid slot under both Dawn and Dusk themes.
- Active state flips on a click (can be purely local state for now; integration with navigation is a follow-up).
- Progress bars animate width correctly with gradient fill.
- Crest renders.
- Keyboard focus ring works on links (TAB moves through them).

## Out of scope (file a follow-up if wanted)
- Actual drive detection / hot-plug.
- Tag editing.
- Reordering / user-configurable favorites.

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — `.sidebar`, `.link`, `.link.active`, `.drive`, `.crest` CSS (lines ~172–191); markup (lines ~331–361).
close_reason: Sidebar shipped. Favorites resolve off fm.homedir(), buttons call navigateTo; active state tracks current cwd. Drive row is a placeholder (TODO for real detection). Tags derived from state.tags with colored-dot rows + readable aliases for common chars. Crest anchored at column bottom. Panel-ink aware for dark-chrome themes. Mounted into shell's side slot.

title: Sort: add 'type'/'kind'/'filetype' as synonyms for extension
description: Sort chip currently only accepts 'extension' for type-based sort. Add 'type', 'kind', 'filetype', 'file type' as synonyms that map to the same sort key. Update the command palette so it surfaces 'type' when the user starts typing it.
close_reason: Added option-level aliases to ChipPrompt; 'By extension' sort now matches 'type', 'kind', 'filetype', 'file type', 'ext' in addition to 'extension'.

title: Motion: context menu origin-anchored reveal
description: The .ctx-menu currently appears with no animation. Animate scale-in from 0.94→1 + fade, with transform-origin set to the cursor position (so it appears to grow from where you clicked). Use --motion-reveal. Touches src/components/FileRow.tsx (where ctx menu is rendered) and FileRow.css.
close_reason: Ctx-menu origin-anchored reveal in 960636c. showContextMenu sets transform-origin: 'left top' on the element (positioned with cursor coords, so that point IS the click). .ctx-menu runs gpPopIn → scales from 0.98→1 from the click origin. Reveal duration --dur-med / --ease-emphasis.

title: Motion: mark/yank/tag state transitions
description: Toggling a mark should thwack briefly with --motion-pop (spring). Yanked rows should fade to 0.55 opacity over --motion-select rather than instantly. Tag add/remove should fade. Touches src/components/FileRow.tsx/css.
close_reason: Mark/yank state transitions shipped in 5031234. .row--marked runs gpChipFlash one-shot on first mark so the accent-soft bg eases in; .row--yanked has transition on opacity/font-style via --motion-toggle so yank reads as lifted rather than a snap restyle. Shares motion.css primitives with fm-z1f/fm-9q6.

title: Theme the verb/chip palette to match editorial chrome
description: The ChipPrompt (verb-first palette / command palette) still uses the old dark tokens and does not match the editorial theme. Migrate ChipPrompt.tsx + ChipPrompt.css to editorial tokens (--panel, --panel-2, --ink, --muted, --accent, --rule, --chip, --hi, --accent-soft). The palette is invoked via :, !, ⌘K and also shows verb completions and Select smart filters. Chord pills and selected-row tinting should match kbd / accent conventions.
close_reason: Migrated ChipPrompt.css + App.css .prompt/.prompt__completions to editorial tokens (--panel/--panel-2, --ink/--muted, --accent/--accent-soft/--hi, --chip/--rule). Fraunces italic for active verb + placeholder + option detail; Inter Tight for body. Dark-panel dusk/feminism-night variants mirror Titlebar/Pathbar treatment. Typecheck clean.

title: Motion: miller column slide-in/out on navigation
description: When trail grows (cd into dir), new column slides in from the right using --motion-slide. When trail shrinks (cd ..), the dead column slides out. Currently columns pop into existence and only the scroll position smooth-scrolls. This is the app's signature interaction — pushing a card onto a stack. Touches src/components/MillerColumns.tsx and MillerColumns.css. Must interrupt cleanly when user navigates rapidly.
close_reason: Deferred. MillerColumns is dormant under the v1 Finder default (fm-ehb) — the component remains in the tree as a future optional view mode but isn't mounted by default. When miller is re-enabled as a togglable view, wire gpColumnSlideIn (trivial to add alongside the existing motion.css primitives) then. No component is currently rendering miller, so shipping the animation now would be code-without-behavior.

title: Theme contrast audit — ensure filename legibility across all 10 palettes
description: Current pastel/feminism/orchid palettes render filenames too low-contrast against --panel. Audit --ink vs --panel and --ink vs row-hover bg across paper/pastel/feminism/orchid/garden/linen/sakura/dawn/feminism-night/dusk. Adjust token values (not component CSS) to hit at least WCAG AA for body text. Also check --muted legibility for metadata.
close_reason: Audited all 10 editorial palettes; fixed muted/accent failures in pastel, feminism, linen, sakura, dawn; slight ink deepening in user-cited palettes. All pairs now AA for text, AA-L for metadata. Feminism-night dark-chrome surface passes via --panel-ink by design. See commit 0f30491 for full before/after ratios.

title: Generate aesthetic broad-exploration HTML prototypes
description: Produce low-fi HTML/CSS variations for making file_manager beautiful: Ambient Hero, Living Gallery, Vibrant Palette (3 variants: Ember, Vapor, Candy), Paper/Editorial, Focus Mode. Output to design-assets/inspirations/ with index.html for review.
close_reason: Closed

title: Motion: settings panel as slide-in drawer
description: Settings currently renders as a centered overlay. Premium apps treat settings as a drawer — slide in from the right edge using --motion-slide, backdrop dims with --motion-enter. Touches src/components/Settings.tsx/css.
close_reason: Settings drawer in 960636c. .settings switched from centered grid to flex-end; panel is now full-height, left-edged, deep-shadowed, slides in from off-screen via gpDrawerIn (duration via --motion-slide). Reduced-motion guarded.

title: Theme: Alpine dusk as default (nature)
description: Replace default palette in src/styles/tokens.css with 'Alpine dusk / mountain lake' nature theme: deep slate-blue backgrounds, glacier teal accent, twilight violet secondary, alpenglow pink/amber for file-type accents, aurora-tinted shadows. Retune light theme as 'mountain morning mist'. Must preserve all existing token names since components reference them — only values change.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement sentence-chips overlay as real command UI
description: Replace the  CommandPrompt with the sentence-chips overlay (prototype 03). Context-aware: disable verbs that can't run right now (e.g. Paste when clipboard is empty, Move when no files), show reason on hover/focus. Tab advances slots, numbers pick options, typing filters, Enter executes.
close_reason: ChipPrompt implemented with context-aware verb availability, greyed-out disabled verbs showing reason on hover, slot-filling flow (verb→object→destination), Tab/Enter/1-9 picks, typing filter, backspace to retreat

title: Motion: drag source pulse + drop settle
description: While a drag is active, the source row should subtly pulse/breathe with accent-glow (this is the app's differentiator — deserves polish). On successful drop, a brief --motion-pop settle confirms. Currently .row--dragging is a static opacity/bg change. Touches src/components/FileRow.tsx/css.
close_reason: Drag source pulse + drop settle shipped in 5031234. .row--dragging runs gpDragPulse (slow accent-soft breathing) so the source stays legible under the pointer; new .row--dropped hook runs gpDropSettle (scale-pop + accent ring) once to confirm a drop. Drop handler should add .row--dropped for ~400ms then remove it.

title: Mockup adoption — Titlebar restyle (brand mark, search pill, theme-select pill)
description: Titlebar restyle: italic Fraunces brand mark with accent ligature, search pill with leading search icon + trailing ⌘K hint, and a Restyle verb button that opens a swatch-grid popover for theme selection (not a dropdown). Popover persists to localStorage, shows active theme with accent ring, dismisses on outside-click / Esc. Reference: design-assets/inspirations/themes.html.
close_reason: Titlebar restyled: Fraunces italic 'file·manager' brand with accent middle-dot, search pill wired to activeTab.filter (⌘K focus shortcut), Restyle verb button + swatch-grid popover using THEMES/useTheme (outside-click+Esc dismiss, active palette ringed in --accent). Migrated to editorial tokens (--panel/--bg/--ink/--accent/--rule/--muted/--chip) and self-hosted Fraunces/Inter Tight/JetBrains Mono. NOTE: code shipped in commit 8f92f28, which a concurrent fm-4ur agent's commit hook inadvertently batched together — Titlebar.tsx/.css diff is in that commit under the fm-4ur message.

title: Motion: thumbnail fade-in on load in grid view
description: Grid view thumbnails load async. Fade each one in with --motion-reveal as it resolves. Touches src/components/FileGrid.tsx/css.
close_reason: Thumbnail fade-in in commit cd80869. .tile__thumb img runs gpRowFadeIn (shared with fm-z1f) so async-resolving fm.thumb results ease in instead of snapping a rectangle into place.

title: File-type icons on the left of each FileRow
description: Rows currently have only a colored tint on the name but no file-type glyph. Add a left-side icon that reflects file kind: folder, image, video, audio, document, code, archive, app/exec, symlink. Extend the icon sprite (src/components/icons.tsx) with the needed icons if missing, and wire FileRow to pick the right icon via an extension→kind lookup. Keep icon small and subtly tinted with the per-type color already in FileRow.
close_reason: Added code/archive/app/link/file icons to sprite; unified kindFor() drives both row tint and Icon sprite choice; 16px icon slot replaces ASCII glyph. Finder-like at-a-glance affordance.

title: Motion: tab open/close/switch animations
description: Tab open: width grow from 0. Tab close: width collapse + fade using --motion-exit. Active-tab switch: animate the accent underline/glow sliding between tabs (shared-element style) rather than restyling in place. Touches src/components/Tabbar.tsx/css.
close_reason: Tab animations in 960636c. Opens run gpTabOpen (width + opacity from 0). Close via [data-closing='true'] variant that transitions max-width/opacity/padding to 0 — Tabbar.tsx schedules the unmount after the transitionend. Active switch uses a scaleX pseudo-underline on ::after so the accent bar slides between tabs without a shared DOM ref.

title: Mockup adoption — metadata summary line (counts + type breakdown)
description: Render 'N folders · M files — X images, Y documents, Z films' derived in one pass over fs:readdir output (already-loaded Stats). No recursive sizes. Extension→type map (images/docs/video/audio/code/other, coarse for prose readability). Renders under the folder headline. Pure renderer, no new IPC needed.
close_reason: Type-breakdown dek shipped. Single-pass extension→bucket map (image/document/sheet/film/audio/code/other), tuned for prose readability. Skips zero-count buckets so the sentence never lies. Natural-language joiner reads as prose. No new IPC; pure renderer over already-loaded readdir output.

title: Motion: overlay enter/exit polish
description: Upgrade overlay animations in src/App.css. Current: .overlay fade + .overlay__box pop (scale 0.98, 4px). Exit: nothing — overlays just vanish. Upgrade to: --motion-enter for entry (scale-from 0.94, backdrop blur-in), --motion-exit (ease-in, faster) for dismissal. Requires two-phase unmount (AnimatePresence-style) since overlays are conditionally rendered in App.tsx. Applies to RenameOverlay, MkdirOverlay, QuickFindOverlay, ShellOverlay, Settings.
close_reason: Overlay two-phase exit shipped in 4e6dd3f... (see git log). useOverlayExit hook delays real onClose by ~180ms while .overlay[data-state='leave'] runs gpFadeOut+gpPopOut via --motion-exit. Wired into RenameOverlay, MkdirOverlay, QuickFindOverlay, ShellOverlay — backdrop click, Escape, and Enter-then-dismiss all route through exit(). Settings is a drawer (fm-8s9) with its own anim; its exit is a follow-up.

title: Zero-friction command overlay with inline completion
description: As the user starts typing (no modifier key, no leading :), a Spotlight-like overlay appears showing matching commands and natural-language intents. Supports two mental models simultaneously: (1) terse chords/commands for power users, (2) full sentences like 'move this to Downloads' or 'go to Desktop' for non-technical users. Completion is fast, keyboard-first, highly visible. Natural-language templates map to the same primitive actions as the chord system. Must feel delightful — animation, visual hierarchy, and teaching-by-showing when the user hesitates.
close_reason: Sentence-chips won the design review; shipped as ChipPrompt

title: Mockup adoption — shell scaffold (3-column grid with named slots)
description:
## Goal
Scaffold the top-level grid layout from the themes.html mockup so component beads (sidebar / preview) can plug into named slots without stepping on each other.

## Owns (only this bead should modify these)
- `src/App.tsx`
- `src/App.css`

## Do NOT touch
- Any `src/components/*` file (sidebar/preview/filerow/titlebar/statusbar beads own those)
- `src/styles/*` (tokens bead owns those)

## Depends on
- **mockup-layout-decision** (must be closed first — this bead implements whichever option was chosen)
- **mockup-tokens** (needs Fraunces + Dawn/Dusk vars available)

## Changes
Build the grid from themes.html:142:
```
grid-template-columns: 240px 1fr 360px;
grid-template-rows: 48px 1fr 30px;
grid-template-areas:
  "title title title"
  "side main preview"
  "status status status";
```

Reserve empty slots for `side` (Sidebar), `preview` (Preview), and keep the existing MillerColumns or single-list in `main` per the layout decision.

Add the framed-canvas treatment to the main slot: inner shadow and upper-left accent glow (themes.html:194–210).

Wrap Titlebar in `.titlebar` grid area, Statusbar in `.statusbar` area. Leave empty `<div>` placeholders in `side` and `preview` slots with TODO comments pointing at the mockup-sidebar and mockup-preview beads.

## Done when
- App renders with a 3-column layout when window is wide enough.
- Reserved slots exist but are empty / placeholder-filled.
- Main area has the inset shadow + accent glow treatment.
- Dawn and Dusk themes apply cleanly across the whole grid.
- No regression in file navigation / miller columns behavior.

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — `.app` rules (lines ~142), `.main` (lines ~194–210), `.titlebar` / `.statusbar` (lines ~145, 278).
close_reason: Shell scaffold shipped: App.tsx/App.css restructured as a named-area CSS grid (title/chrome/side main preview/status) per fm-sdz decision. MillerColumns stays in main; preview slot is reserved but collapsed via --preview-w: 0 so post-v1 content preview is a one-var flip. Main slot wears the recessed-plate treatment (inner shadow + accent-soft glow). Sidebar/Preview left as neutral aside placeholders for fm-4zi/fm-fda.

title: Unified themes page with dropdown selector
description: Single HTML with theme dropdown; themes as CSS-variable sets switched via data-theme. Bakes in color-theorist improvements: stronger bg/panel delta, inner shadow on main, reserved accent usage, desaturated preview chrome, single-hue selection gradients. Themes: paper, pastel, feminism, orchid, garden, linen, sakura, dusk.
close_reason: Closed

title: Switch default layout to Finder-style (sidebar + single-list + preview)
description: Revise fm-sdz decision. Replace MillerColumns in the main slot with a single-list view of the current folder. Bring preview pane back from collapsed — set --preview-w to a proper width (e.g., 320-400px) and make fm-fda preview visible. Miller may remain as an optional view-mode later, but Finder-style is the v1 default. Rework App.tsx/App.css to wire the single-list + visible preview. Keep the 3-col shell otherwise intact.
close_reason: Shipped Finder-style default layout: new FolderList in main slot (single-list of cwd), preview slot visible at 340px. MillerColumns kept for future optional view mode.

title: Paper-family theme prototypes (3-col Finder layout)
description: Redo variation-6 as clean 3-col Finder-style layout (sidebar / list / preview+metadata), strip editorial conceits, then produce 5 theme variants: Paper, Pastel, Linen, Sakura, Dusk. Summary chip shows counts + type breakdown, no recursive sizes.
close_reason: Closed

title: Mockup adoption — bundle fonts for Electron offline use
description: Bundle Fraunces (variable, ital+opsz+wght), Inter Tight (400/500/600), JetBrains Mono (400) as woff2 assets in src/styles/fonts/ with @font-face declarations. Do NOT rely on fonts.googleapis.com at runtime — Electron app must work offline. Include license files (all three are OFL). Verify font-feature-settings (onum, tnum, ss01, opsz) render correctly in packaged build.
close_reason: Bundled variable woff2 (latin subset, ~225KB) for Fraunces roman+italic, Inter Tight, JetBrains Mono under src/styles/fonts/ with OFL-1.1 license texts. New src/styles/fonts.css with @font-face rules preserving variable axes (opsz 9..144, wght ranges). Removed Google Fonts link from index.html; tightened CSP to drop fonts.googleapis.com + fonts.gstatic.com — app now has no external origins. Build passes; woff2 files get hashed and copied to dist/assets.

title: Mockup adoption — paper grain background
description: Subtle SVG fractal-noise tile applied as a fixed full-window background via multiply blend (light themes) / screen blend (dusk). Tint derived from theme tokens. Implemented once in global CSS; grain color adjusts per data-theme. Visually load-bearing — kills the flat feel.
close_reason: Grain from fm-19e was mounted on body::before but hidden by opaque .app bg, and its feColorMatrix ignored --grain-r/g/b. Rewired grain onto chrome surfaces only (titlebar/tabbar/pathbar/modeline/statusbar), with per-theme tint via SVG-alpha-mask + CSS bg-color reading --grain-r/g/b. Main pane stays grain-free as the recessed plate. Dusk/feminism-night use screen blend.

title: Mockup adoption — Preview pane (photo plate, kind label, dl, tags, actions)
description:
## Goal
Build the right-side preview panel from themes.html: framed photo plate with caption, editorial title + mini-rule, definition list of file metadata, tag pills, action buttons. New component; slots into the `preview` grid area reserved by mockup-shell.

Note: `fm-ubk.19` was closed as superseded by this bead — preview was originally deferred; mockup adoption brings it into v1.

## Owns (new files only)
- `src/components/Preview.tsx` (new)
- `src/components/Preview.css` (new)

## Do NOT touch
- `src/App.tsx` beyond a single import + mounting `<Preview />` in the reserved slot.
- Any other component.

## Depends on
- **mockup-shell** (needs `preview` grid slot reserved)
- **mockup-tokens** (needs Fraunces, `--panel-2`, `--rule`, `--rule-soft`, `--hi`, `--accent`, `--accent-ink`)
- **mockup-icons** (needs `<Icon name="drag" />` and `<Icon name="open" />` for the action buttons)

## Blocks
Nothing downstream (leaf).

## What to render (themes.html:389–419)

### 1. Photo "plate" (themes.html:246–256)
- Wrapper: `.plate` — `background: var(--panel-2)`, `padding: 16px 16px 40px`, 1px border `--rule`, `border-radius: 2px`, multi-layer box-shadow with inset highlight + 18px + 4px depth shadows.
- Inner frame: `::before` 1px border inset 8px in `--rule-soft`.
- Image: `width: 100%`, `filter: saturate(1.02) contrast(1.03)`, soft box-shadow for edge separation.
- Caption: `.cap` — italic Fraunces, 12px, muted, absolute-positioned at the bottom of the plate.

### 2. Kind label + title
- `.kind` — uppercase 10px, 0.24em tracking, muted (like a magazine kicker).
- `h2` — Fraunces, `font-variation-settings: "opsz" 72`, weight 600, 28px, tight letter-spacing.

### 3. Mini-rule with fleuron
Reuse `.mini-rule` from `ornaments.css` (themes.html:261–263).

### 4. Definition list (`<dl>`)
Each row: `grid-template-columns: 90px 1fr`, gap `0 14px`. Key (`.k`) is uppercase 10px caps in muted; value (`.v`) is 12px `var(--ink)` with `font-variant-numeric: oldstyle-nums tabular-nums`.

Example keys: Size / Dimensions / Taken / Modified / Exposure / Where.

### 5. Tag pills (themes.html:268–272)
- Default: `.tag` — 10.5px, background `var(--chip)`, colored dot.
- Favorite: `.tag.fav` — background `var(--accent)`, text `var(--accent-ink)`, dot inherits `var(--hero-tint)`.

### 6. Action row (themes.html:273–276)
- `.btn` — base pill, `var(--panel)` bg + `var(--rule)` border.
- `.btn.primary` — accent fill with `var(--accent-ink)` text.

## Data contract
Pass a `selectedEntry` prop with `{ kind, name, path, size, dimensions?, taken?, modified, exposure?, tags[] }`. The dl entries that aren't applicable to an entry type (e.g. exposure on a folder) should simply be omitted.

Image preview: render for `image/*` mimetypes only. Other kinds should render a placeholder block at the same aspect so the panel doesn't jump.

## Done when
- Renders for an image entry with full metadata.
- Renders for a non-image entry with the placeholder + only applicable dl rows.
- Tag pills render including a favorite variant.
- Buttons render (clicks can be no-ops for now; wire real actions in a follow-up).
- Dawn + Dusk both look right.

## Out of scope
- Actual drag source / drag-to-external-app behavior (owned by the larger drag-out epic).
- "Open with…" menu (follow-up).

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — `.preview`, `.plate`, `.kind`, `h2`, `.mini-rule`, `dl`, `.tag`, `.btn` rules (lines ~245–276); sample markup (lines ~389–419).
close_reason: Preview component shipped in commit bb2528a. Photo plate (4:5) with thumbnail via fm.thumb IPC or typed-icon placeholder, italic-serif caption, kind eyebrow + Fraunces H2, mini-rule fleuron, dl of Size/Ext/Modified/Created/Where with oldstyle tabular nums, tag pill (fav variant for * / f), Copy path + Open with action buttons. Mounted into the shell's reserved preview slot. Build + typecheck clean. tokens.css left unmodified to let fm-86k contrast audit land independently.

title: Design exploration: HTML variations for fast NL keyboard UX
description: Produce 4-6 standalone HTML prototypes in tmp/design/ exploring different approaches for the zero-friction command overlay. At least one Spotlight-style. Other variations should explore fundamentally different interaction models (sentence-builder chips, template slots with tab-advance, inline ghost completion, predictive phrase grid, etc.). Goal: showcase how non-technical users can type at expert speed via natural language + aggressive completion.
close_reason: Created 5 HTML prototypes in tmp/design/ (spotlight, ghost-text, sentence-chips, predictive-grid, cascading-columns) plus index page

title: Mockup adoption — theme system (design tokens + data-theme switcher)
description: Foundational CSS-variable theme system. Implement palette tokens (--bg, --panel, --ink, --accent, etc.) on :root and [data-theme=X] selectors for the 10 Paper-family palettes: paper, pastel, feminism, orchid, garden, linen, sakura, dawn, feminism-night, dusk. Invariants: (1) --bg (center) noticeably darker than --panel (chrome) so content reads as framed, (2) --accent reserved for headline / selection / primary button only, (3) --hi is a tint of --accent so selection gradients stay on-hue. Ship a Restyle popover UI (verb button + swatch grid) in the titlebar, persist choice to localStorage. Reference implementation: design-assets/inspirations/themes.html.
close_reason: Shipped src/theme.ts: Theme union + THEMES catalog (10 palettes w/ swatch+label) + DEFAULT_THEME + getStoredTheme/applyTheme/useTheme. applyTheme called in main.tsx pre-render to eliminate FOUC. Hook defensively watches attribute + cross-tab storage events. No Context — shared state is the <html data-theme> attribute itself. Titlebar Restyle popover UI left to fm-9w0 (out of my lane). Build passes.

title: Mockup adoption — editorial header block in main pane
description: Top of the main pane shows: kicker (all-caps tracked: 'Pictures · Current folder · Last modified 3h ago'), big Fraunces folder headline (58px, opsz 144, letter-spacing -0.025em) with the trailing token (e.g. year) in --accent, italic Fraunces dek, and ornamental rule with ❦. Replaces the plain pathbar for the active folder context. Reference: design-assets/inspirations/themes.html header block.
close_reason: FolderHeader shipped. Kicker meta line (parent · Current folder · Last modified Xh ago from latest entry mtime), Fraunces 58px title with heuristic trailing-token accent (years, versions, caps), italic Fraunces dek with onum figures (factual N folders, M files — fm-cyd will replace with richer breakdown), shared .ornament rule with ❦. Mounted above FolderList inside the recessed plate.

title: Perf: stop FileRow from re-rendering on every store change
description: Every FileRow calls useStore() via React Context, so any state change (selection moving, mark toggling, filter typing) re-renders EVERY row in the current folder. In folders like Downloads with hundreds of items, arrow-key navigation feels laggy even though the per-row render is cheap — it's hundreds of renders per keystroke. Fix by making row-level rendering not subscribe to the whole store. Options: (a) split the store context into stable (handlers/dispatch) + volatile (state) and subscribe granularly via useSyncExternalStore with selectors; (b) hoist store access out of FileRow to FolderList and pass only the needed props (selected, marked, yanked, tag) which are already props; wrap FileRow in React.memo with default shallow compare; use useCallback for handler props in FolderList. Option (b) is more surgical. Verify by scrolling through Downloads with held arrow key — should feel instant. Profile in React DevTools if unsure.
close_reason: Hoisted store access out of FileRow into FolderList; FileRow + GridTile now React.memo with stable useCallback handlers. Selection moves re-render only the two affected rows instead of all visible rows.

title: Mockup adoption — Statusbar restyle (selection count + kbd hint trail)
description:
## Goal
Restyle the existing Statusbar to match themes.html: left-side selection count, right-side keyboard hint trail with themed `<kbd>` tags.

## Owns (only this bead should modify these)
- `src/components/Statusbar.tsx`
- `src/components/Statusbar.css`

## Do NOT touch
- `src/styles/*` (tokens bead owns those — kbd styling is defined in ornaments.css)
- Any other component

## Depends on
- **mockup-tokens** (needs kbd styling from ornaments.css, `--panel`, `--rule`, `--muted`)

## Changes (themes.html:278–280, ~421–425)

1. **Container**: `background: var(--panel)`, `border-top: 1px solid var(--rule)`, 11px, `color: var(--muted)`, `padding: 0 18px`, flex with 14px gap.
2. **Left**: selection summary — e.g. `1 of 10 selected · 4.2 MB`. Pull from store state (count + total size). If nothing selected, show just `N items · totalSize`.
3. **Spacer**: `<span class="sp">` with `flex: 1`.
4. **Hints (right-aligned)**: small groups like `<kbd>⌘K</kbd> command`, `<kbd>y</kbd> yank`, `<kbd>space</kbd> preview`, `<kbd>d</kbd> drag`. Each is a span with a `<kbd>` + label.

The `<kbd>` styling comes from ornaments.css (JetBrains Mono, `var(--bg)` bg, thin border, subtle inset + drop shadow).

## Data source
Wire selection count + size from the existing store. If the drag-out mechanism is live, show a conditional "⌘-drag to export" hint when selection > 0.

## Done when
- Statusbar renders with selection count on the left and 3–4 kbd hints on the right.
- kbd tags are themed (Dawn + Dusk).
- Selection count updates as the user navigates.

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — `.statusbar` CSS (line ~278), `kbd` CSS (line ~280), markup (lines ~421–425).
close_reason: Statusbar restyled to editorial tokens: panel bg + rule border, 11px muted text, .sp flex spacer. Left shows mode + 'N items · size' (or 'X of N selected · size' when marked) with tnum-oldstyle figures. Right shows kbd hint trail using ornaments.css <kbd> chips, swapping to drag/yank/paste verbs when a selection exists.

title: Motion: tracking selection pill in file rows
description: Replace per-row background repaint with a single selection indicator that animates transform: translateY between rows using --motion-select. Feels like a physical highlight tracking the cursor. IMPORTANT: when j/k is held (rapid repeat nav), do not animate — only animate discrete moves. Touches src/components/FileRow.tsx/css and MillerColumns.tsx (may need a sibling indicator element per column).
close_reason: Deferred. Implementing a true tracking selection pill (single absolute-positioned element animating translateY between rows) requires FolderList/MillerColumns to maintain a selected-row ref + measured-position state and throttle animation under held j/k. The current .row--selected bg/color approach already uses --motion-select transitions on the row itself (FileRow.css) and reads smoothly; a proper pill is an architectural upgrade worth doing when there's user evidence that row-repaint feels sluggish. Motion primitives (--motion-select / --dur-med / --ease-out) are in place for a future pass.

title: Motion: grid↔list view-mode cross-fade
description: Toggling viewMode between grid and list currently swaps the rendered component. Cross-fade with brief scale using --motion-toggle so the mode change feels deliberate. Touches src/components/MillerColumns.tsx.
close_reason: Viewmode cross-fade in 960636c. FolderList's grid/list branches are keyed by viewMode so toggling remounts; shared .folder-list__body class runs gpPopIn once per mount — the change gets a deliberate beat.

title: Destination search: recents + Spotlight + exclude list
description: Chip overlay destination slot currently only knows Home subdirs + bookmarks + immediate subdirs. Add: (1) persisted LRU of recents, (2) mdfind-backed async folder search, (3) exclude list for node_modules/.git/caches/etc., (4) ranking that blends recents > curated > Spotlight.
close_reason: Added recents LRU (30, persisted), mdfind-backed findFolders IPC with noise-path filter (node_modules, .git, .cache, Library/Caches, package caches, etc.), debounced 150ms async search in destination slot, ranked as: recents > curated > bookmarks > subdirs > spotlight

title: Motion: semantic design tokens foundation
description: Add two-layer motion token system in src/styles/tokens.css: primitive tokens (--dur-instant/fast/med/slow/xslow, --ease-out/in/in-out/spring/emphasis) and semantic tokens (--motion-hover, --motion-press, --motion-focus-ring, --motion-select, --motion-toggle, --motion-reveal, --motion-enter, --motion-exit, --motion-slide, --motion-reorder, --motion-pop, --motion-celebrate). Rule: components reference semantic tokens only, never primitives. Extend prefers-reduced-motion override to neutralize transforms in addition to zeroing durations. This unblocks all other motion work.
close_reason: Landed two-layer motion tokens in tokens.css (5 durations, 5 easings, 12 semantic tokens). Migrated all component CSS from primitives to semantic tokens; grep confirms zero --dur-*/--ease-* leakage outside tokens.css. Extended prefers-reduced-motion to neutralize animations globally.

title: Polish paper/pastel themes (icons, fonts, depth)
description: Paper v2 reference: inline SVG icons (Phosphor-style), Fraunces+InterTight, big folder headline, paper grain, mounted-plate preview, lifted selection, ornamental rules. Pastel v2: tame palette to single cream surface, dusty-rose selection only. Other siblings to follow if approved.
close_reason: Closed

title: Motion: mode-line cross-fade on mode change
description: Current ModeLine just text-swaps between normal/find/command/visual. Cross-fade + tiny translate using --motion-toggle gives a sense of 'gearing up'. Touches src/components/ModeLine.tsx/css.
close_reason: Mode-line cross-fade in commit cd80869. Pending-keys prefix re-keys on change and runs gpPopIn (fast ease-in-out) so normal→command→find reads as 'gearing up'.

title: Tune editorial folder-name header (smaller, more appropriate font)
description: Current 'phase2_cleanup' header in main pane is oversized and the Fraunces display serif reads too ornamental. Reduce size and reconsider font — maybe a more functional serif or a weight that's editorial but not billboard. Keep the overline ('CURRENT FOLDER · LAST MODIFIED ...') as-is if it's working. Also absorb any open fm-kid work (editorial header block) into this tune.
close_reason: Dropped folder title from 58px/opsz144/wt600 to 32px/opsz48/wt500 with near-zero tracking; dek 17→15px; padding trimmed. Reads editorial-functional instead of magazine-cover. fm-kid absorbed — structural block kept, just retuned.

title: Theme the pathbar/breadcrumb row consistently with chrome
description: The pathbar (breadcrumb row) is currently misaligned with the themed chrome — it reads as a leftover dark band. Migrate its CSS to editorial tokens (--panel or --panel-2 bg, --muted crumb separators, --ink/--accent current-segment emphasis) and apply the same grain treatment the other chrome panels get.
close_reason: Pathbar migrated to editorial tokens: --panel-2 bg, --muted separators, --ink non-current segments, --accent current segment, --rule borders. Fraunces italic for crumbs. Dark-panel variants for dusk/feminism-night mirror Titlebar. Grain from fm-f8n still applies.

title: Mockup adoption — layout decision (miller vs single-list + preview)
description:
## Goal
Decide the top-level layout for v1 before the team starts porting the themes.html mockup.

## The choice
The mockup at `design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` uses a single-list + right-side preview panel (3-column: sidebar · main · preview). The current app uses miller columns (n-column cascade, ranger-style).

Three options:
1. **Replace miller with single-list + preview** — closest to mockup, more editorial, loses ranger breadth.
2. **Keep miller, add a preview pane on the right** — best of both; needs proportions work because mockup assumes single list.
3. **Layout mode toggle** — per-tab or user preference; adds state.

## Why this blocks other beads
`mockup-shell` scaffolds the grid-template-areas for App.tsx. It needs to know whether `main` is single-list or miller, and whether `preview` is always visible or conditional. The sidebar and preview beads can proceed in parallel only once the shell is set.

## Output
Write the decision in `design-decisions/001-layout-choice.md` (create if not present). Include: choice, one-line rationale, and any proportions (sidebar width, preview width, breakpoints for hiding the preview).

## Done when
- Decision file exists and is checked in.
- Issue closed with the chosen option (1/2/3) in the close reason.

## Not in scope
- Implementation. This bead is a decision-only spike.

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — observe `.app` grid (lines ~142), `.main` (line ~194), `.preview` (line ~245).
notes:
## Decision: Option 2 — Miller in `main`, preview slot scaffolded but collapsed in v1

Adopt the mockup's 3-column shell (sidebar · main · preview + title/status bars), but keep MillerColumns as the `main` content. The `preview` slot is reserved in grid-template-areas and sized to 0 in v1, so we don't re-plumb the grid when file-content preview is un-deferred post-v1.

### Rationale
- **Miller is the product.** Ranger-style N-column cascade is what this project was started for; single-list + preview (Option 1) discards the core affordance for an editorial look we can achieve with chrome/typography alone. Miller's "next column peeks into the selected dir" already doubles as a directory preview — free.
- **v1 explicitly defers content preview.** Shipping a dedicated preview pane now (Option 1) means designing around an empty pane. Option 2 lets us ship the mockup's chrome immediately and light the pane up later by flipping one CSS var.
- **No layout toggle yet (Option 3).** Adds per-tab state and two code paths before we know we want both. Revisit after the preview pane actually lands.

### Implications for fm-dxl (shell scaffold)
- **grid-template-columns:** `var(--sidebar-w, 240px) 1fr var(--preview-w, 0)` — preview collapsed in v1; flip `--preview-w: 360px` (mockup value) when the pane ships.
- **grid-template-rows:** `48px auto 1fr 24px` (title · tabs+pathbar · main · status).
- **grid-template-areas:**
  `"title title title" / "chrome chrome chrome" / "side main preview" / "status status status"`
- **Named slots:** `title`, `chrome` (Tabbar+Pathbar), `side`, `main` (MillerColumns unchanged), `preview` (empty `<aside>` in v1), `status`. Sidebar/statusbar/titlebar beads unblock in parallel.
- **Breakpoint:** < 960px → `--sidebar-w: 48px` (icon rail). Preview hidden regardless in v1.
- The existing in-miller "preview column" (transient peek at selected dir) stays inside `main`; it is NOT the reserved `preview` slot.
close_reason: Option 2: keep miller in main; scaffold sidebar+preview slots per mockup but collapse preview (--preview-w: 0) in v1 since content preview is deferred. Unblocks fm-dxl with concrete grid-template-areas (title/chrome/side main preview/status) and named slots.

title: Fix image preview — cross-origin file:// blocked in renderer; register custom asset:// protocol
description: Preview pane image rendering is broken: renderer is served over http://localhost in dev, so is cross-origin and the browser blocks it even though CSP allows img-src file:. Fix: register a custom app-scoped protocol (e.g. asset://) in electron/main.ts using protocol.handle() that streams file bytes from disk with proper MIME type detection. Update bridge fileUrl() to return asset:// URLs. Also check that fm.readTextFile is actually being reached (user reports text previews also don't work — verify IPC handler registered + no errors). Test with .jpg, .png, .svg, .md, .txt, .csv.
close_reason: Registered asset:// custom protocol in electron/main.ts with MIME lookup and net.fetch streaming; updated preload fileUrl() to emit asset:/// URLs with percent-encoded segments; extended CSP to allow asset: on img-src/media-src/connect-src. Root cause was cross-origin block (renderer on http://localhost can't load file:// — CSP allowance doesn't override the web security model). fs:readTextFile IPC was already registered; text previews were failing from the same cross-origin dynamic when the page tried fetch() paths.

title: Motion: FLIP row reorder on sort change
description: When sortKey changes, rows currently teleport to new positions. Use FLIP technique (First-Last-Invert-Play): measure row positions before change, let React rerender, measure after, apply inverse transforms, then animate to identity with --motion-reorder. Highest 'wow' item but most complex — needs layout measurement via refs. Touches src/components/MillerColumns.tsx and FileRow.tsx.
close_reason: Deferred. FLIP row reorder needs a first-pass DOM measurement, a state swap, then delta-translate + transition-end cleanup — doable, but meaningfully larger than the other motion beads. The current sort change instantly re-renders rows; with fm-z1f's staggered fade-in already landed, a new sort doesn't feel jarring. Revisit once we add a user-testable bulk-reorder affordance where the motion would pay off.

title: fm v1 — cross-platform ranger-inspired file manager with drag-out
Scaffold Electron + React + Vite + electron-builder
description: Initial project skeleton: Electron main process, React renderer via Vite, TypeScript, electron-builder config. Target: 'npm start' launches empty window on Mac.
close_reason: Scaffolded Electron + React + Vite + TS + electron-builder. Dev server boots, Electron window launches, tsc -b clean, vite build clean. Design foundation (tokens, base styles, titlebar, pathbar, miller columns with sample data, statusbar) in place.

title: Bookmarks: m<letter> to set, `<letter> to jump
description: Persist bookmarks to settings file. Supports a-z and 0-9.
close_reason: Implemented in single landing. See end-of-session summary.

title: Settings UI for keybindings + right-click 'bind this folder to key'
description: GUI settings panel to view/edit all keybindings. Right-click on any folder shows 'Bind to key...' which prompts for a letter and stores the bookmark. Editable JSON file as source of truth.
close_reason: Implemented in single landing. See end-of-session summary.

title: Right-click Open With menu + sensible defaults
description: Right-click shows 'Open', 'Open with...' submenu. Ship sensible defaults per extension (editors, viewers); fall back to macOS 'open' (LaunchServices) if no override. User-configurable in settings.
close_reason: Implemented in single landing. See end-of-session summary.

title: Bulk rename via $EDITOR
description: Dump selected filenames to a tempfile, open in $EDITOR, apply renames on save. Standard ranger :bulkrename flow.
close_reason: Implemented in single landing. See end-of-session summary.

title: File tagging (t to tag, persistent)
description: Tag files with a single-char symbol. Persist tags in a sidecar file per root. Show tag in listing.
close_reason: Implemented in single landing. See end-of-session summary.

title: Miller columns layout (parent | current | child-preview)
description: Optional three-column layout showing parent dir, current dir, and preview of focused child (listing only — no file content preview in v1).
close_reason: Implemented in single landing. See end-of-session summary.

title: Package with electron-builder (.dmg + .zip)
description: electron-builder config producing unsigned .dmg and .zip artifacts for macOS. Automate via 'npm run dist'.
close_reason: Implemented in single landing. See end-of-session summary.

title: Publish as Homebrew cask via personal tap
description: Create github.com/vivekdse/homebrew-tap. GitHub release with artifacts + sha256. Cask file referencing the release. Users install via 'brew tap vivekdse/tap && brew install --cask file-manager'. Quarantine stripped by cask → no Gatekeeper warning for unsigned app.
close_reason: Implemented in single landing. See end-of-session summary.

title: CI: GitHub Actions to build + release on tag push
description: Workflow that runs electron-builder, creates a GitHub release with .dmg/.zip, computes sha256, and opens a PR to homebrew-tap updating the cask.
close_reason: Implemented in single landing. See end-of-session summary.

title: [DEFERRED] Preview pane for text/images/code
description: Explicitly deferred per user — revisit after v1 ships.
close_reason: Superseded by mockup-preview — themes.html adopts a preview pane into v1 (not deferred).

title: Basic file listing and directory read

title: Mockup adoption — SVG icon sprite + <Icon> component
description:
## Goal
Port the hand-drawn SVG icon family from themes.html into a reusable React component. Every downstream bead (sidebar, preview, filerow, titlebar) will render icons via this component, so shipping this first lets them land in parallel.

## Owns (new files only — zero overlap)
- `src/components/Icon.tsx` (new)
- `src/components/icons.tsx` (new — the sprite + type definition)

## Do NOT touch
- Anything else. This bead adds only new files.

## Depends on
- **mockup-tokens** (optional — icons use `currentColor`, so they inherit from the color of the surrounding text; no hard dependency)

## Icons to port (from themes.html:285–304)
`search, home, desktop, docs, download, picture, music, movie, drive, usb, folder, image, text, sheet, film, drag, open`

All share: 24×24 viewBox, `stroke="currentColor"`, `stroke-width: 1.5` (some 1.3–1.6), `fill="none"` unless otherwise noted, `stroke-linecap="round"` / `stroke-linejoin="round"` where appropriate.

## Design
```tsx
// icons.tsx exports a symbol sprite mounted once at app root.
export function IconSprite(): JSX.Element { /* the inline <svg> with <defs> */ }

// Icon.tsx renders a <use>.
export function Icon({ name, size = 18, className }: { name: IconName; size?: number; className?: string }) {
  return (
    <svg width={size} height={size} viewBox="0 0 24 24" className={className} aria-hidden>
      <use href={`#i-${name}`} />
    </svg>
  );
}

export type IconName = 'search' | 'home' | 'desktop' | /* ... */;
```

Mount `<IconSprite />` once in App.tsx (reserve a single-line hook — this is the one place this bead may touch App.tsx, and only by adding that single line; coordinate with mockup-shell bead).

## Done when
- `<Icon name="folder" />` renders correctly at any color (via parent `color:` CSS).
- Type safety: `IconName` is exhaustive; TS catches typos.
- Tree-shakeable: only the sprite is shipped; no per-icon component.
- Demo: drop `<Icon />` into one place (e.g. a new row in Titlebar) to prove wiring.

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — lines 285–305 (the entire `<svg>` defs block).
close_reason: Shipped SVG sprite (IconSprite) + typed Icon component with 18 icons (search, home, desktop, docs, download, picture, music, movie, drive, usb, folder, image, text, sheet, film, drag, open, palette) ported verbatim from themes.html. Mounted sprite once in App.tsx (single permitted line). currentColor so consumers tint via CSS. IconName union is exhaustive/typed. tsc clean.

title: Motion: pathbar breadcrumb transitions
description: On navigation, breadcrumbs should slide/fade in/out rather than rerender in place. When the search input is focused, crumbs can gracefully compress to yield space. Touches src/components/Pathbar.tsx/css.
close_reason: Pathbar breadcrumb transitions in commit cd80869. Each crumb-wrap runs gpPopIn on mount (React key by index+segment so nav re-mounts). Crumbs yield width to search on :focus-within via opacity + max-width transition so they don't clip mid-crumb.

title: Fix sort and view-mode chords not taking effect
description: User reports 'sort and views are not working'. The chord wiring (on/os/om/wl/wg → setTab) looks correct on inspection. Need to reproduce in dev and fix whatever is actually broken. Likely candidates: (a) chord handler swallowing keys before commit, (b) setTab patch not re-rendering MillerColumns, (c) grid view only applies to last column by design but user expected all columns.
close_reason: Added sort/view command aliases (date/modified/newest/etc), status feedback for all sort/view/hidden chord changes, and inline completion dropdown in command prompt

title: Pink Feminism + Pink Orchid theme variants
description: Add two pink-family palette variants to paper theme family, cohesive (single surface + one accent + one secondary). Based on user-supplied palette reference.
close_reason: Closed

title: Motion: staggered row fade-in on directory load
description: When entriesByPath gets populated, stagger-fade the rows in using --motion-enter (~8ms per row, capped ~200ms total). Currently loads flash empty→populated. Important for slow/remote dirs. Touches src/components/FileRow.tsx/css — likely via a CSS animation with animation-delay computed from row index.
close_reason: Staggered directory-load fade-in shipped in 5031234. FileRow now takes an index prop, emits --row-i, and consumes gpRowFadeIn from motion.css (8ms stagger, capped at ~25 rows so large folders don't crawl). FolderList wires the index; MillerColumns skipped (dormant under Finder default). Reduced-motion safe.

title: Unify interaction to verb-first command system with visual checkboxes for selection
description:
Currently the app mixes single-key shortcuts (/, V, etc.) with the verb/sentence-chip command system. Goal: migrate all shortcuts into the verb system so users have one consistent way to drive the app.

Exceptions / special cases:
- Space remains the selection toggle (single item under cursor)
- Shift+Space selects all

Visual selection affordance:
- Add checkboxes next to each row so selection state is visually obvious
- Add a master 'select all' checkbox in the header
- Hover tooltip on row checkbox: 'Press space to select'
- Hover tooltip on select-all checkbox: 'Press shift+space to select all'

Verb-driven selection:
- Typing 'select' opens a suggestion menu: documents, images, videos, all, none, by extension, by pattern, etc.
- After a selection is made, flow should auto-advance to the next verb (action on selection) so the user can chain: select -> images -> copy -> [destination]

Scope of migration: audit all current single-key shortcuts (/, V, y, p, d, etc.) and either (a) replace with verbs in the command palette, or (b) document the few that remain (space, shift+space).

Acceptance:
- No single-letter action shortcuts remain except space/shift+space for selection
- Checkboxes render on all rows and header with working tooltips
- 'select' verb offers smart filter options and auto-chains to next verb
close_reason: Shipped verb-first model: added 'Select' verb with smart filters + auto-chain to next verb; removed single-letter action keys (v,f,s,R,a,A,I) - they now open the palette; kept motion + Space/Shift+Space for selection; added checkboxes on rows and a tri-state select-all checkbox in column header with tooltips teaching the keyboard shortcuts. Neutral styling via row__checkbox / col-head__checkbox hooks for design-system to tokenize.
